reviews.review_msg: great instructor, detailed weekly schedule, caring, clear explanation.

Dr. Rimrott is very caring. She goes over slides in great detail and answers questions well. She also gives out lots of extra practice, which is needed for this course. I really enjoyed having her as a professor!

This couse has a lot of work weekly, but she always have a clear to-do list for students to follow. It actually doesnt take that long to complete. Everthing on Moodle is organized pretty well. The course is not super hard but you do need to spend some time on it. There are office hours twice a week for u to reach out live. Shes pretty nice.
She is the best instructor I have ever had

really responsible, the best the most complicate Moodle page I've ever seen! and really helpful! but her class is really difficult. Moreover, she is a weird person. She always show she is a German and how pound she is because she is a German although she do not have relationship with Germany at all. What a funny guy!
To anyone reading this review on this page, get off this page and look for another prof. to take a class with. You will regret taking this class. WORST. PROF. EVER.

Just finished ling220 with Dr Rimrott, got good score again! For students who has taken ling 110, this course is definitely a good choice. Lectures are attractive and interactive as usual, so does Annes great patience!
Her class is REALLY DIFFICULT, I don't recommend this class. She's not helpful outside of class. If I need help, I'm asked to ask other students. The content is very hard.

One of the best Profs ever, Dr Rimrott is really a professional LING educator. The best lecture note ever, she kindly encourages all of her students join and carries all of them. Be careful don't lose any detail in her lecture notes and pay attention on her words, if you do, u don't have problems with A. If not, u will just get average score.

Anne gives tremendously engaging lectures, is accessible, and passionate about her field. However, some of the exams force students to really think outside some of the material. While I think that's admirable and ambitious, it feels risky for a 220 course if students haven't taken a previous LING course. Definitely take on all of the readings.

WORST PROF EVER! she does not test on course material. Went into the midterm feeling very prepared, studied off of her study guide, and I barely managed to pass it. Definitely would not recommend this course with her.
Anne is a very good prof.  Her grade scale and tests are fair and her lectures are engaging.

Anne is one of the best at SFU.  She is well-organized, explains concepts very clearly, and her tests are always fair.  She makes long lectures engaging, and while she's not a stand-up comic like some profs, she has a good sense of humour.  100% would recommend taking LING 100 with her.
Organized and speaks intelligently. Powerpoint notes are helpful. Anne makes the 3 hour lectures interesting. Would recommend.
She is good

WORST PROF EVER!  she does not test on course material. Went into the midterm feeling very prepared, studied off of her study guide, and I barely managed to pass it. Definitely would not recommend this course with her.

Anne is awesome! Took LING 100 with her and currently in her LING 220 class. She really does her best to make lectures interesting and understandable for everyone. Showing up to lectures are a must. Marking and tests are fair; some things will need extra review.

Anne is an awesome lecturer and I would definitely attend her lectures again if she does teach any other LING courses. There's group work for marks every lecture so even though she won't check attendance, you won't get the grades if you don't show up. Attend lecture to understand the course content better. Very nice and funny.
Best professor ever! Her lectures are engaging, I never got bored, and if you need help she goes above and beyond to talk to you!

My favourite prof so far. 3 hour lectures breeze by because she's a really clear and engaging educator. Knows her stuff and is passionate about linguistics! Very approachable and kind. Provides a lot of material to help you succeed in her course and is quick to answer questions. You will learn a lot & have fun!

Anne is the best prof I've had so far, and I'm not in ling. Her lectures are long but engaging, she posts all material online. There are workshops and office hours if you need help. Weekly homework and discussion. Graded group worksheet each class. Participation matters. She posts study guides. If you read the book & attend lecture you'll pass.
Great prof! She makes sure that you understand the course materials. No final, only 3 midterms (multiple choice)  and online participation. She also provides study guide for the exams.

Amazing teacher! Made a 3 hour lecture very entertaining (videos, discussions, etc...). Exams are very fair - if you understand and read the material you will do well.  Very helpful as well; she quickly responded to questions asked on Canvas.

I thought she was amazing! 3 hour long lectures, but broken up with 2 ten minute breaks, videos and group discussion in class. Textbook was interesting and engaging. Subject anyone can relate to, because we all use language. No final exam, 3 noncumulative midterms instead. Attendance not mandatory but you miss weekly quizzes if you skip class.

Highly recommend taking this class with Anne. Interesting, engaging, and very little commitment. Lecture are long but Anne recgonizes this and give you lots of breaks and really strives to brake up the class with discussions and video. Anne has inspire me to pursue a degree in linguistics and I'm so happy to have had her as a prof!!

I found Anne to be excellent at explaining the course content. There wasn't any material presented that didn't make any sense after the lecture. She really started at the begining. She also realizes that sitting through a 3 hour lecture is hard and so gives you lots of breaks.

Amazing prof. She marked 100+ student assignments BY HERSELF during the TA job action . She is clear and knows her stuff, as well as making the class very multicultural. However, she puts in effort and expects it from students too, so weekly homework and test are quite tough, but doable if you study reasonably.

Anne clearly explained all concepts and was always available to give extra help.  her exams were completely unfair. Instead of  the final exam just testing the material learned, the questions were of a higher difficulty level than expected for an introductory course. I would not recommend this course unless exams are changed to be more fair.

Anne's interest in the subject is super high, she's hilarious, and absolutely amazing when it comes to explaining tough concepts. Couldn't have passed the class without her c: would 100% have her as a prof again

Great Prof. She is very knowledgeable and is passionate about her subject. She made me want to study more linguistics, although it's fairly far outside of my major. Besides her excellent teaching abilities she is also a great person in private.

She's very nice, and she's passionate and knowledgeable about linguistics. Lecture slides are taken from the textbook, and she explains them in a way that makes the textbook almost unnecessary. The tests are fair if you PRACTICE applying the concepts a lot. She helps you practice during the lectures, so make sure to attend.
She has a great personality, and the class can be fun if you are in a small class.
Don't take  this class with her, you'll definitely regret it.
Interesting course

She seems like she'd be really nice, but for an introduction to a subject you need to leave a little leeway for misunderstandings. Her tests were way to hard given the material she was teaching and what was in the textbook, her slides and what she said. Not a fair grader. She wanted us to be upper level students not be novices.

I don't know why some of the reviews say the exams are hard... I thought they were pretty fair if you studied decently. Her lectures were clear enough that I didn't read the textbook. She's willing to help if you have any questions and is also receptive to feedback. Overall, I her class was pretty laid back and I enjoyed learning.
She was nice professor during class; she interacts with people. Lecture slides were pretty clear. The midterm was fair but the final was really hard.

Very organized prof but this class is NOT a GPA booster. What we learn in lecture is understandable but her assignments and exams are really HARD. She her self says no one can get an A in her class. You will learn quite a bit in her lecture as she is great at teaching but applying what you've learnt to the actual course work is difficult !
Final is very hard, and don't konw why

I think Anne was really nice and you can tell she is interested in linguistics. Overall, the course was fairly easy although I did not receive the grade I had hoped for. Midterms are pretty much based on lectures. I would say you could probably get away without reading the textbook.

Lectures are clear and helpful, but exams are extremely hard compared to assignments and examples. She's receptive to feedback from students though. Interesting material but probably not a GPA booster.
Nice lady, fair assignments, impossible exams. Avoid.
stupid prof and final is so hard.

Class is very interesting and prof is OK.However, exams are extremely hard (class average for the final is even less than 55%), and the course grading scheme is very hard either, so even you are on average of the class, you are very likely to get a C or even C-... Not recommended to take it if Anne is teaching it because your GPA would drop

Horrible prof. Tells class that she hates getting emails from us. Extremely hard exams. Got A+'s on the 2 big assignments, yet barely managed to pass the midterm and final. DO NOT TAKE THIS COURSE WITH HER.

Exams are extremely hard. The concepts are straightforward but the questions she asks are ridiculous. All multiple choice questions for the midterm, but literally every answer could be correct. The grading scheme is extremely high as well and she does not curve the course. Overall I really disliked her as a prof and would avoid if possible

I like her way of teaching but her exams are hard and the average was C for this semester which is not reasonable for a 1st year course and she should've curved the course .. she is nice but gets mean quickly.. Also her grading scheme is different from all other courses where B+ is 81-85,B is 76-80, and B- is 71-74 ..

Really friendly and personable. The first midterm was really hard but a lot of the students talked to her and she changed it for the next one. The only thing I didn't like was the high grading system.
Midterm1 was hard .Midterm2 and Final were pretty straightforward.  I do not like her grading system, so its better to avoid her. she was really helpful when I asked some question though..

The content of this course was  straightforward but the Prof was mediocre. My main gripes with her come from  her lack of clarity when explaining new or confusing concepts, and her draconian grading system. 79-84 was a B- if I recall correctly. I scored a 75 in the class landing me a C+  much to my dismay. Subpar Prof; take course w/ someone else.

Midterm was deceptively easy compared to the final, a lot of people were taken by surprise as there seemed to be a lot more in detail questions and terminology than we covered in class.. frustrating to see such a style change from one exam to the next.. profs need to have some sense of consistency!

I had her for LING220 and thought she was wonderful! She can be a bit monotone, but I found her lectures helpful although her notes are quite broad. A lot of the other students in the class I talked with seemed to like her as well and found the course relatively easy. Assignments and midterm were easy, although the final was trickier.
the course is easy but the exam is HARD. exams are M/C but you have to read evey thing in the books(tow books) and lectures.. If you want to lose your GGPA then take a course with her :)

Very clear lecture, and she is a good teacher that she will listen to the students and try to make changes, she shows her respect! Not like other old and conceited pro., and exams contain only M/C questions which are very straight forward! Study through the exam study guide, you will get a good grade!

Very easy going prof. Course was all multiple choice. Prof is very organized and well spoken. She is genuinely interested in teaching linguistics and is happy when students get something out of her course.
If you study all the notes that she gives you then the test seems very straightforward. Lectures are valuable.

There are only multiple choice questions on the midterm, and she said most people did well (I also found the midterm really easy). Assignments which have few questions are also easy. Overall, I would say this course is a GPA booster :) I liked her lectures, so didn't skip at all, but you can get all important information from the book.

I really liked this course and liked her lectures. However, she explains a lot of materials, so you should have some interest in linguistics. If you wanna know something about linguistics, you can enjoy this course:)
I believe this course is one of the easiest courses at SFU! Exams are fair, and you will have no problem if you do all practice questions before exams:) You should take this course with her.
ridiculous prof.  really made a l00 level course so much harder. tested way too specific.
Arrogant person. Explain everything so unclear and confuse. Midterm is extremely specific. Make a easy GPA booster course much more difficult

Treats her class like children and her exams are ridiculously hard. Tolerates ZERO noise in the lecture. No sense of humor. If she sees you talking you will be called out. Everyone I talk to in this class hates it. I am interested in the material but how she teaches it is awful. She needs to lighten up and change her attitude toward students.

Treats class like grade 2 children behaviourally, words exams at way higher maturity level than 100 level course should be. She'll wait for everyone to completely cease talking before starting the lecture, and will stop to tell you off if she hears any chatting during.
Very unclear.
Stupid prof, worst I see in sfu, must be first year teaching.

The way she teaches is so unclear, dull and boring. Very hard projects. Althoght she has some handy notes, but doesn't make the situation better. This is an absolutely easy course but she just makes it like a 300 level course! Unbelievable, never take her course or you will be regretted for sure, it drags your gpa down! Office hour is useless too!!
